metadata.greeting: Get ready for a fantastic weekend in Hackney! I've found some amazing activities perfect for a 3-year-old, from exploring city farms to enjoying interactive museum exhibits. Let's make this weekend one to remember with hands-on fun and exciting adventures!

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: Reading corner and diverse book-pairing service.
activities.whyItWorks: Free, drop-in, caters to a wide age range, and encourages a love for reading.

transport.fromHackney: Various bus routes and Overground to Hackney Downs or Hackney Central.
transport.tip: Check term times before heading out.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: Interactive museum designed specifically for children, with collections of toys, design and childhood related items.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on activities, play areas, and exhibits designed to spark creativity and imagination in young children.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Bethnal Green station, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check for special events or workshops on their website.

activities.title: Mudchute Park and Farm
activities.description: One of the largest city farms in London, with a variety of animals and open spaces.
activities.whyItWorks: Kids love seeing the animals, and there's plenty of room to run around and play. Free entry is a bonus!

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Mudchute station.
transport.tip: Wear wellies if it's been raining!

activities.title: Spitalfields City Farm
activities.description: A small, community-focused city farm with animals and gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: A more intimate farm experience, great for younger children. Check out their Easter activities.

transport.fromHackney: Buses or Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check for seasonal events and workshops.

activities.title: Discover Children’s Story Centre
activities.description: Immersive play spaces known as Story Worlds and a Story Garden playground.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers Baby Storytelling plus crafts and singalong time for babies and toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground line to Stratford station.
transport.tip: Check website for specific event timings.

activities.title: Easter Family Community Celebration
activities.description: Easter Family Community Celebration
activities.whyItWorks: Could be good for family

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Woodford

activities.title: Easter Egg-Stravaganza
activities.description: Live show, egg hunt, easter bonnet parade, face painting, arts and crafts, café and plenty of delicious treats!
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of fun activities

transport.fromHackney: Train to Putney

activities.title: Sound Explorers at Young V&A
activities.description: Sound Explorers
activities.whyItWorks: Free

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Bethnal Green

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, amazing animals and the wonders of the natural world.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaur-themed hunts, hands-on science experiments, and seasonal storytelling sessions during Easter.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Green Park, then Piccadilly Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Book a free timed entry ticket in advance to avoid queues.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Discover how the world works with interactive exhibits and fascinating displays.
activities.whyItWorks: The Garden in the basement is an interactive space for younger children to discover construction, water, light and sound through play.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Green Park, then Piccadilly Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Check for family-friendly events and workshops.

activities.title: British Museum
activities.description: Explore world cultures and history through fascinating artifacts and exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: Egyptian galleries with mummies, activity backpacks with puzzles, games and activities available.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Oxford Circus, then Central Line to Tottenham Court Road.
transport.tip: Download a family trail map before you go.

activities.title: Cutty Sark Easter Egg Trail
activities.description: Take part in an Easter Egg Trail throughout the historic ship.
activities.whyItWorks: Discover fascinating facts about Cutty Sark's epic voyages, while you explore the decks

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Greenwich
transport.tip: Most suitable for young explorers age five+ and their families

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Search for giant Easter eggs hidden around the grounds.
activities.whyItWorks: Each egg reveals a secret letter, encouraging curious minds to work together to uncover the code hidden throughout the Zoo.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Euston, then Northern Line to Camden Town.

activities.title: Changing of the Guard, Buckingham Palace
activities.description: A traditional ceremony with marching bands and guards.
activities.whyItWorks: Visually engaging and a classic London experience, though can be crowded.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Victoria.
transport.tip: Arrive early to secure a good viewing spot.

activities.title: Sky Garden
activities.description: Indoor garden with panoramic views of London.
activities.whyItWorks: The greenery and plants make it a sensory experience, and the views are amazing.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Bank, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance online. Free entry

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.description: Explore natural history, musical instruments, and anthropology exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: Aquarium, Butterfly House and various specialised exhibitions including the 'Robot Zoo'.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Forest Hill.

activities.title: Family Easter Makes Workshop at Kenwood
activities.description: Family Easter Makes Workshop
activities.whyItWorks: Crafts

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Gospel Oak, then walk or bus

activities.title: Easter at Camden Market
activities.description: Entertainment from Blossom the Lion and Sita the circus acrobat, explore an Easter trail of 3D artworks, get crafty at the Bonnets and Bunnies workshop, plant spring seeds and bulbs, have your face painted, and join the Cheeky Meeky Family Rave.
activities.whyItWorks: Free

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Camden Road

activities.title: Islington Easter Trail 2026 | Family Easter Activity
activities.venue: David Astburys - Estate Agents Islington N1
activities.description: Family Easter Activity
activities.whyItWorks: good for family

transport.fromHackney: Overground

activities.title: Hampton Court Palace – Easter Events
activities.description: Visit Hampton Court Palace for Easter-themed egg hunts and seasonal workshops.
activities.whyItWorks: Explore historic rooms, gardens, and the palace grounds while participating in fun activities for children

transport.fromHackney: Train from Liverpool Street to Hampton Court

activities.title: Harry Potter and the Cursed Child
activities.description: Twenty years after Harry, Ron, and Hermione saved the wizarding world, they're joined by a brave new generation.
activities.whyItWorks: A magical theatre experience

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Leicester Square

activities.title: Easter Egg Hunt
activities.address: Ham Street, Ham, Richmond, Surrey, TW10 7RS
activities.description: Enjoy fun activities, game stations and an exciting egg trail through this glorious garden in Richmond.
activities.whyItWorks: Great family fun in a lovely setting

transport.fromHackney: Train to Richmond, then bus

activities.title: Easter Quest at Eltham Palace and Gardens
activities.price: Paid, in addition to paid admission
activities.description: Embark on a fun-filled adventure quest around the house and gardens. Follow the Easter trail, hunt for eggs and answer questions along the way to earn a delicious chocolate reward.
activities.whyItWorks: Good family fun

transport.fromHackney: Train to Eltham

activities.title: The Very Hungry Caterpillar Trail at Walthamstow Wetlands
activities.description: Children aged 8 and under are invited to discover a variety of illustrated animals nestled amongst the blooming habitats of the reserve, identify the letter on each one and complete the secret word in the activity booklet.
activities.whyItWorks: Good family fun

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Walthamstow
